name: About the consignment (purpose of import)
pageTitle: What is the main reason for importing the consignment? - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: About the consignment
heading: What is the main reason for importing the consignment?

structure.text: What is the main reason for importing the consignment?
purpose radio group: Internal market / Transhipment or onward travel / Transit / Re-entry (govuk-radios--conditional)
Internal market — hint: The consignment is intended for sale or use in Great Britain (England, Scotland or Wales).
Purpose in the internal market (nested radios: Animal feedingstuff / Human consumption / Other)
Transhipment or onward travel — hint: The consignment will stay within the same port or airport in Great Britain. It will change means of transport, then travel to a third country.
Transit — hint: The consignment will move through Great Britain. It will enter and leave through different ports or airports, then travel to a third country.
Exit border control post (select); When the consignment will leave Great Britain — date input + time input; Transited country (select) + Add another country; Destination country (select)
Re-entry — hint: The consignment is authorised for re-entry or includes rejected exports that are re-entering Great Britain.
Save and return to hub (variant — rendered when editing/amending an existing notification from the hub; inferred, AboutTheConsignmentPage.ts:50-52 + ched-p-manipulation.spec.ts:49)

govukComponents.usage: Return to the previous page in the journey.
Section caption 'About the consignment' above the H1.
Primary 'purpose' radio group with conditional reveals; plus the nested 'internal-market' radio group inside the Internal market reveal.

Four fieldsets in the DOM (verified: document.querySelectorAll('.govuk-fieldset').length === 4). Three carry a visible <legend>: 'Purpose in the internal market' (--m, also an H3 fieldset heading); 'When the consignment will leave Great Britain' (--s) for the date input; visually-hidden 'Time entry:' for the time input. The fourth wraps the main 'purpose' radio group and has no <legend> element in the DOM (only 3 legends are present) — the H1 sits outside/labels it rather than being a fieldset legend.
Destination country (transhipment), Exit border control post (transit), Transited country (transit), Destination country (transit) — all conditional on purpose.
Estimated arrival at port of exit — a day/month/year date input and an hour/minutes time input, both revealed under Transit.
Primary 'Save and continue' submit button. A second 'Save and return to hub' submit is present in the amend/edit-from-hub variant (inferred, not in this trace).

Six hints (verified: document.querySelectorAll('.govuk-hint').length === 6): one under each of the four top-level purpose radios (internalmarket-hint, transhipment-hint, transit-hint, re-entry-hint), one under the date input (estimated-arrival-at-port-of-exit-date-hint) and one under the time input ('24 hour format, for example, 14 50'). The nested internal-market radios (Animal feedingstuff / Human consumption / Other) carry no hints.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A button styled to look like a link that adds another 'Transited country' select row within the Transit reveal, so a consignment can list multiple transited countries.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not a GOV.UK Design System component — a bespoke link-styled button plus client-side JS to clone the country select. The add/remove-item pattern is not in govuk-frontend.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: No direct component. Rebuild as a server-rendered repeatable-item pattern (add/remove) using standard govuk Select rows and a govuk-button--secondary 'Add another', or the GOV.UK 'add another' pattern from the design system community backlog.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom classes hooking a JavaScript calendar date-picker enhancement onto the estimated-arrival date input, plus per-segment hook classes.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: The govuk-date-input is standard, but the '--picker' overlay is a bespoke IPAFFS JS widget layered on top — not part of govuk-frontend, and a known accessibility/overlay complication (per workspace memory, IPAFFS date-picker overlays intercept clicks).
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Use the plain GOV.UK Date input (three text fields) with no JS picker overlay; that is the Design System's recommended pattern for a known date.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Name-derived wrapper classes on the radio group containers (used as JS hooks for the conditional show/hide logic).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Harmless custom hook classes, not visual styling; flagged only for completeness. They imply client-side JS drives the conditional reveals in addition to the govuk-radios--conditional behaviour.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-radios--conditional already handles conditional reveal natively; no bespoke hook needed.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: accessible-autocomplete exit-BCP variant (combobox 'BCP or Port of exit' / textbox 'Point of exit' + role=option suggestions)
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: An alternative type-ahead control for choosing the exit border control post / point of exit — a combobox with a suggestion list rather than the plain govuk-select seen in the trace.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Accessible-autocomplete is a common IPAFFS enhancement layered over a native select; it is not a core govuk-frontend component and adds JS + a11y surface. Not observed in the corpus trace (which rendered the plain select), so this is an inferred variant only.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: GOV.UK accessible-autocomplete component (progressive enhancement over a plain govuk-select) if a type-ahead is genuinely needed for the BCP list; otherwise keep the plain select.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: AboutTheConsignmentPage.ts:54-56 (textbox 'Point of exit'), :82-84 (option suggestions), :86-92 (combobox 'BCP or Port of exit')

fields.name: purpose
fields.label: What is the main reason for importing the consignment?
fields.evidence: db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 25 (name=purpose; values internalmarket/tranship/transit/import; test checks 'Re-entry' -> value 'import', id radio-reimport). Page object corroborates the CHED-P purpose set: radioInternalMarket (AboutTheConsignmentPage.ts:10), radioReEntry (:6), radioTransit (:30); workflow default purpose='reEntry' with 'transit' the only other CHED-P branch (ched-p-workflows.ts:101-102,304-308); Internal market + Animal feedingstuff driven in the non-transit workflow (ched-p-workflows.ts:692-694). NB: the Transhipment reveal renders in the DOM but no CHED-P/IUU workflow or test ever selects it — never exercised.

fields.name: internal-market
fields.label: Purpose in the internal market
fields.evidence: db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 25 (name=internal-market; values animal/human/other; legend/H3 'Purpose in the internal market'). Corroborated: radioAnimalFeedingstuff (AboutTheConsignmentPage.ts:18) + radioHumanConsumption (:14) driven at ched-p-workflows.ts:693-694 (Animal feedingstuff). 'Other' exposed in DOM but never driven by a test.

fields.name: third-country-transhipment
fields.label: Destination country
fields.evidence: db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 25 (select name=third-country-transhipment, 251 options — country reference data, sourced from a reference-data service in the new app). No test drives the transhipment branch, so options are trace-confirmed but the field is never exercised end-to-end.

fields.name: bcp-transit-third-country
fields.label: Exit border control post
fields.evidence: db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 25 (select name=bcp-transit-third-country, 34 options — BCP reference data). Corroborated by page object: selectExitBcpTransit = select#bcp-transit-third-country (AboutTheConsignmentPage.ts:38-40); also reachable by accessible name via selectExitBorderControlPost combobox 'Exit border control post' (:94-96). INFERRED requirement (test assertion): HOLYHEAD (GBHLY) must appear in this Transit exit-BCP list — ched-p-holyhead.spec.ts:11-26 asserts it (the observed 34-option snapshot was truncated and did not include it).

fields.name: estimated-arrival-at-port-of-exit-date-day / -month / -year
fields.label: When the consignment will leave Great Britain
fields.hint: For example, 27 3 2023. This must be within 7 days of arrival in Great Britain.
fields.evidence: db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 25 (three text inputs day/month/year, legend 'When the consignment will leave Great Britain', hint id estimated-arrival-at-port-of-exit-date-hint). Corroborated: fillDate() sets day/month/year to today+3 (AboutTheConsignmentPage.ts:98-111), consistent with the 'within 7 days' hint.

fields.name: estimated-arrival-at-port-of-exit-time-hour / -minutes
fields.label: Time entry:
fields.evidence: db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 25 (hour/minutes text inputs; legend visually-hidden 'Time entry:'; Hour/Minutes date-input labels; hint '24 hour format, for example, 14 50'). Corroborated: inputHour/inputMinutes textboxes 'Hour'/'Minutes' filled '12'/'00' (AboutTheConsignmentPage.ts:70-75,109-110).

fields.name: transit-third-countries (id transit-third-countries-last)
fields.label: Transited country
fields.evidence: db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 25 (select name=transit-third-countries, 254 options; repeatable via 'Add another country' link-button — country reference data)

fields.name: third-country-transit
fields.label: Destination country
fields.evidence: db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 25 (select name=third-country-transit, 251 options — country reference data)

fields.name: save-and-return-to-hub (secondary submit, variant)
fields.label: Save and return to hub
fields.conditionalOn: amend / edit-from-hub context (an existing notification being changed), not the linear create flow
fields.evidence: AboutTheConsignmentPage.ts:50-52 (btnSaveAndReturnToHub, role button 'Save and return to hub'); driven at ched-p-manipulation.spec.ts:49 when re-selecting the purpose after amending country of origin. Never rendered in the create-flow trace db2d277c.

fields.name: bcp-temporary-admission (variant — CHED-A only, not IUU)
fields.label: Exit border control post (temporary admission of horses)
fields.conditionalOn: purpose = Temporary admission horses (CHED-A live-animals branch)
fields.evidence: AboutTheConsignmentPage.ts:42-44 (select#bcp-temporary-admission). Belongs to the CHED-A temporary-admission-of-horses purpose, which is not part of the CHED-P / IUU purpose set and was never rendered in the corpus. Recorded for completeness; almost certainly out of scope for the IUU rebuild.

openQuestions: Evidence trace db2d277c... is a CHED-P (Fish) notification, not a dedicated IUU trace. This 'About the consignment / purpose of import' page is shared across CHED-P (and CHED types); it is a strong proxy for the IUU journey since IPAFFS files IUU fishery products as CHED-P. Confirm the IUU rebuild keeps the same four-purpose model (Internal market / Transhipment / Transit / Re-entry) or simplifies it — the new standalone IUU app may not need all four purposes.

The shared page object exposes extra purpose radios that belong to OTHER CHED types and were never rendered in the CHED-P/IUU corpus: 'Non-internal market' (AboutTheConsignmentPage.ts:22-24), 'Transfer of ownership – Rescue' (:26-28) and 'Temporary admission horses' (:34-36), plus a select#bcp-temporary-admission (:42-44). These are CHED-A live-animals options. Confirm none of them apply to IUU — the IUU purpose set should be the four CHED-P purposes only.

The 'purpose' radio group and all conditional sub-fields showed required=false in the DOM (client-side/JS-driven validation, not the HTML required attribute). No error state was captured in this passing trace, and no CHED-P test asserts a validation-message string for this page, so all validation copy ('Select a reason' / country / date / time messages) is a gap — mine an error trace or the legacy ValidationMessages in a later integration wave.

Behaviour rule (inferred, ched-p-manipulation.spec.ts:47-49): selecting the purpose is CLEARED when the country of origin is amended upstream, forcing the user to re-answer this page. Confirm whether the IUU rebuild should reset purpose on an upstream change, or preserve it.

The Transhipment ('Destination country', third-country-transhipment) branch renders in the DOM but is never driven by any CHED-P workflow or test — its options are trace-confirmed but the branch is un-exercised. Likewise 'Human consumption' and 'Other' under Internal market are rendered but never selected. Confirm these branches are still required for IUU.

An accessible-autocomplete variant of the exit-BCP control exists (combobox 'BCP or Port of exit' / textbox 'Point of exit' with role=option suggestions — AboutTheConsignmentPage.ts:54-92) alongside the plain govuk-select seen in the trace. Confirm which control the IUU rebuild should use (recommend a plain govuk-select, or the GOV.UK accessible-autocomplete if type-ahead is needed).

INFERRED requirement from ched-p-holyhead.spec.ts:11-26 — HOLYHEAD (GBHLY) must be present in the Transit exit-BCP list; the observed 34-option snapshot was truncated. Confirm the full exit-BCP set against the reference-data service.

The page runs an axe accessibility scan in the CHED-P a11y suite (ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:54-56, titled 'About The Consignment Page') — the rebuilt page must pass WCAG 2.1 AA; the bespoke date-picker overlay and link-button 'Add another' are the main a11y risks to retire.

The 'Transited country' select is repeatable via a bespoke 'Add another country' link-button (add-another pattern). Confirm max cardinality and whether the new app models transited countries as a list.

The date input uses a custom '--picker' JS overlay (date-picker-* classes) on top of govuk-date-input; recommend the new app drop the picker overlay (known click-interception issue) and use the plain three-field GOV.UK Date input.
url is inferred — not directly observed; the snapshot page is a frozen /snapshot/ URL. Confirm the real route path.
